key_insight: A static 5D PCYS interpolation table, periodically re-linearized, can approximate VPSC-based irradiation-driven viscoplastic behavior accurately enough to accelerate FEM simulations of zirconium reactor components.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster multi-scale structural analysis of spacer grids and fuel cladding in Code_Aster, making long-term reactor component assessments more tractable for engineering-scale studies.
method: Built a 5D interpolation-table surrogate for instantaneous viscoplastic response
Coupled periodic recalibration with first-order Taylor linearization to track radiation-induced microstructural drift
Benchmarked the surrogate in FEM against full VPSC-FEM simulations under continuous high-dose irradiation
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that relative errors stayed below 1% for representative deformation paths, while structural spacer-grid simulations showed meaningful computational savings.
watch_item: The approach depends on periodic recalibration and a precomputed database, so accuracy may degrade if irradiation paths or loading conditions move outside the calibrated surrogate range.
paper_title: Accelerating Multi-scale Simulations of Nuclear Components via PCYS Interpolation Tables
